devanagari: भक्तिहीनपुरुषाणां निष्ठायाः प्रतियुगं पूजाविधानस्य च भेदवर्णनम् । श्रीराजोवाच । भगवन्तं हरिं प्रायो न भजन्त्यात्मवित्तमाः । तेषामशान्तका...
english_devnagari: bhaktihīnapuruṣāṇāṃ niṣṭhāyāḥ pratiyugaṃ pūjāvidhānasya ca bhedavarṇanam śrīrājovāca bhagavantaṃ hariṃ prāyo na bhajantyātmavittamāḥ teṣāmaśāntakāmānāṃ kā niṣṭhāvijitātmanām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Nimi) enquired:
O sages, foremost among those who have realized the Ātman! What is the condition and fate of those persons who do not mostly adore Lord Hari and whose -desires are still unsatiated and whose minds and senses uncontrolled?

devanagari: श्रीचमस उवाच । मुखबाहूरुपादेभ्यः पुरुषस्याश्रमैः सह । चत्वारो जज्ञिरे वर्णा गुणैर्विप्रादयः पृथक् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīcamasa uvāca mukhabāhūrupādebhyaḥ puruṣasyāśramaiḥ saha catvāro jajñire varṇā guṇairviprādayaḥ pṛthak

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Camasa replied:
From the mouth, arms, thighs and feet of the Supreme Person were severally originated the four varṇas (classes of society) along with the Āśramas (Stages in life). According to their predominant characteristic qualities (viz. Sattva, Rajas and Tamas and their combinations), they were distinctly classified as Brāhmaṇa, Kṣattriya, Vaiśya and Śūdra.

devanagari: य एषां पुरुषं साक्षात् आत्मप्रभवमीश्वरम् । न भजन्त्यवजानन्ति स्थानाद्‍भ्रष्टाः पतन्त्यधः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ya eṣāṃ puruṣaṃ sākṣāt ātmaprabhavamīśvaram na bhajantyavajānanti sthānād‍bhraṣṭāḥ patantyadhaḥ

[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: Out of these, those who do not adore the Supreme Ruler, their direct Originator, either through ignorance or through negligence of that Great Person, lose their present status and fall down into hell.

devanagari: दूरे हरिकथाः केचिद् दूरे चाच्युतकीर्तनाः । स्त्रियः शूद्रादयश्चैव तेऽनुकम्प्या भवादृशाम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: dūre harikathāḥ kecid dūre cācyutakīrtanāḥ striyaḥ śūdrādayaścaiva te
translation: Women, persons from Śūdra and such other classes (who are denied the opportunity and hence) are away from (the places or chances of) hearing the stories of Hari and also from listening to the songs of the excellent qualities of Hari,[1] deserve compassion from sages like you. (It behoves you to guide them in the path of Devotion.)
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Or: are far from i.e. cannot approach those who extol the excellences of the Lord (in Kīrtana).

devanagari: विप्रो राजन्यवैश्यौ वा हरेः प्राप्ताः पदान्तिकम् । श्रौतेन जन्मनाथापि मुह्यन्त्याम्नायवादिनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vipro rājanyavaiśyau vā hareḥ prāptāḥ padāntikam śrautena janmanāthāpi muhyantyāmnāyavādinaḥ
translation: But Brāhmaṇas, Kṣattriyas and Vaiśyas, by virtue of their original birth, and second birth through their Upanayana (investiture of the sacred thread) ceremony (become eligible for studies, performance of sacrifices, etc.), stand in the proximity of Lord’s feet. But even they are deluded (to become attached to the fruit of their Karmas) by accepting the Arthavāda (apparent and superficial meaning) of the Vedas.

devanagari: कर्मण्यकोविदाः स्तब्धा मूर्खाः पण्डितमानिनः । वदन्ति चाटुकान् मूढा यया माध्व्या गिरोत्सुकाः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: karmaṇyakovidāḥ stabdhā mūrkhāḥ paṇḍitamāninaḥ vadanti cāṭukān mūḍhā yayā mādhvyā girotsukāḥ

[nominative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ muh class 4 verb]

translation: These persons are ignorant of the method of performing Karmas (without getting themselves involved in the fruits of the acts), being conceited fools, they regard, themselves as the learned ones (and hence do not seek guidance of others for the technique of performing Karmas without getting bound by them). These fools being fascinated and impatient by the sweet words of the Vedas (promising happiness in heaven) indulge in sweet talk.

devanagari: रजसा घोरसङ्कल्पाः कामुका अहिमन्यवः । दाम्भिका मानिनः पापा विहसन्त्यच्युतप्रियान् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: rajasā ghorasaṅkalpāḥ kāmukā ahimanyavaḥ dāmbhikā māninaḥ pāpā vihasantyacyutapriyān
translation: Due to the influence of Rajas, they are ruthless in their designs; are lustful and revengeful like serpents. These wicked, conceited hypocrites laugh at the real devotees who are beloved of Hari.

devanagari: वदन्ति तेऽन्योन्यमुपासितस्त्रियो । गृहेषु मैथुन्यपरेषु चाशिषः । यजन्त्यसृष्टानविधानदक्षिणं । वृत्त्यै परं घ्नन्ति पशून् अतद्विदः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vadanti te gṛheṣu maithunyapareṣu cāśiṣaḥ yajantyasṛṣṭānavidhānadakṣiṇaṃ vṛttyai paraṃ ghnanti paśūn atadvidaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Addicted to sexual pleasures, they adore (and cajole) women. Living in houses dominated with craving for conjugal pleasures, they talk to each other of their (present and prospective) achievements (or they wish each other to be blest with wives, children, prosperity). They perform sacrifices without observing the correct procedure or distribution of (adequate) food or giving of (the due) sacrificial fees. They kill animals for personal appetite unmindful of the consequences of such destruction of life.

devanagari: श्रिया विभूत्याभिजनेन विद्यया । त्यागेन रूपेण बलेन कर्मणा । जातस्मयेनान्धधियः सहेश्वरान् । सतोऽवमन्यन्ति हरिप्रियान्खलाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śriyā vibhūtyābhijanena vidyayā tyāgena rūpeṇa balena karmaṇā jātasmayenāndhadhiyaḥ saheśvarān sato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Being elated with the pride of wealth, prosperity, lineage, learning, munificence, personal beauty, physical power and superior aptitude for action, these wicked people get blinded and despise not only righteous devotees beloved of Lord Hari, but also the Supreme Lord Himself.

devanagari: सर्वेषु शश्वत् तनुभृत्स्ववस्थितं । यथा खमात्मानमभीष्टमीश्वरम् । वेदोपगीतं च न श्रृण्वतेऽबुधा । मनोरथानां प्रवदन्ति वार्तया ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sarveṣu śaśvat tanubhṛtsvavasthitaṃ yathā khamātmānamabhīṣṭamīśvaram vedopagītaṃ ca na śrṛṇvate manorathānāṃ pravadanti vārtayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Persons lacking in the wisdom do not care to listen what is explicitly sung in the Vedas about the Supreme Lord—that he is the eternal principle permeating everything like the sky and is the beloved-most Indwelling soul in all embodied beings. They interpret the Vedas suitably as sanctioning their desires (for women, wine, etc.).

devanagari: लोके व्यवायामिषमद्यसेवा । नित्यास्तु जन्तोर्न हि तत्र चोदना । व्यवस्थितिस्तेषु विवाहयज्ञ । सुराग्रहैरासु निवृत्तिरिष्टा ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: loke vyavāyāmiṣamadyasevā nityāstu jantorna hi tatra codanā vyavasthitisteṣu vivāhayajña surāgrahairāsu nivṛttiriṣṭā
translation: It is the natural tendency of creatures to indulge in sexual intercourse, and enjoy non-vegetarian food and wine. No injunction for these is necessary from the Veda. The Veda provides a check over this by allowing sexual intercourse with one’s married wife (at the proper time of the period), eat meat at the end of a sacrifice and drink wine during the Sautrāmaṇi sacrifice (but never otherwise). The real intention of the Vedic injunctions (in these matters) is to make a person abstain from, them.

devanagari: धनं च धर्मैकफलं यतो वै । ज्ञानं सविज्ञानमनुप्रशान्ति । गृहेषु युञ्जन्ति कलेवरस्य । मृत्युं न पश्यन्ति दुरन्तवीर्यम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanaṃ ca dharmaikaphalaṃ yato vai jñānaṃ savijñānamanupraśānti gṛheṣu yuñjanti kalevarasya mṛtyuṃ na paśyanti durantavīryam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: The only object of wealth is to bear fruit, viz. dharma. It leads to spiritual knowledge and direct realization of the soul and supreme peace (mokṣa). But people utilise their wealth for the family or personal comfort and fail to take cognisance of the god of death, the formidable and inexorable enemy of their bodies.

devanagari: यद् घ्राणभक्षो विहितः सुरायाः । तथा पशोरालभनं न हिंसा । एवं व्यवायः प्रजया न रत्या । इमं विशुद्धं न विदुः स्वधर्मम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: yad ghrāṇabhakṣo vihitaḥ surāyāḥ tathā paśorālabhanaṃ na hiṃsā evaṃ vyavāyaḥ prajayā na ratyā imaṃ viśuddhaṃ na viduḥ svadharmam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The smelling of wine (in the Sautrāmaṇi sacrifice) is laid down as its intake (and no drinking of liquor is permitted); a beast is to be (symbolically) offered to the deities (by touching it) in sacrifices, but no killing for meat-eating is allowed; similarly, sexual intercourse (with one’s wife) is to be indulged in for procreation but not for sexual enjoyment. But people do not understand this pure course of their own dharma.

devanagari: ये तु अनेवंविदोऽसन्तः स्तब्धाः सदभिमानिनः । पशून् द्रुह्यन्ति विश्रब्धाः प्रेत्य खादन्ति ते च तान् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ye tu anevaṃvido paśūn druhyanti viśrabdhāḥ pretya khādanti te ca tān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ stabh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ stabh class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ stabh class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ stabh class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ stabh class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ stabh class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ khād class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ khād class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ khād class 1 verb]

translation: But those wicked persons who do not know the real nature of Dharma, and being obstinate and haughty, consider themselves to be righteous and kill beasts without any remorse and are reciprocally eaten by those very beasts in the next world in hell.

devanagari: द्विषन्तः परकायेषु स्वात्मानं हरिमीश्वरम् । मृतके सानुबन्धेऽस्मिन् बद्धस्नेहाः पतन्त्यधः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dviṣantaḥ parakāyeṣu svātmānaṃ harimīśvaram mṛtake sānubandhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

[nominative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: These godless people hate Lord Hari—their very indwelling self who abides in the bodies of others as well (as their Soul); and fixing their attachment to their mortal body and its relatives (wives, sons, etc.), they fall down deep into hell.

devanagari: ये कैवल्यं असम्प्राप्ता ये चातीताश्च मूढताम् । त्रैवर्गिका ह्यक्षणिका आत्मानं घातयन्ति ते ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ye kaivalyaṃ asamprāptā ye cātītāśca mūḍhatām traivargikā hyakṣaṇikā ātmānaṃ ghātayanti te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han ], [vocative plural from √ han ], [accusative plural from √ han ]

translation: Those who have not grasped the correct knowledge of Reality and have crossed the stage of total ignorance (by possessing imperfect knowledge) regard themselves as non- momentary (permanent), and pursue the three objectives of human life (Dharma, Artha and Kāma); such persons (who thus follow a suicidal path) ruin themselves.

devanagari: एत आत्महनोऽशान्ता अज्ञाने ज्ञानमानिनः । सीदन्त्यकृतकृत्या वै कालध्वस्तमनोरथाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: eta ātmahano sīdantyakṛtakṛtyā vai kāladhvastamanorathāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sad class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sad class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sad class 1 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Such people shrouding their Self in ignorance and with their desires unrequieted, mistake ignorance (i, e. Karma) for knowledge. Being thwarted in acheiving their objects and their hopes and wishes being frustrated by the Time-Spirit they ruin themselves (and suffer misery)[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
asuryā nāma te lokā andhena tamasā''vṛtāḥ /
tāñs te pretyā'bhigacchanti ye ke cātmahano janāḥ //
  —Īśa. Up.

devanagari: हित्वा अत्यायारचिता गृहापत्यसुहृत् स्त्रियः । तमो विशन्त्यनिच्छन्तो वासुदेवपराङ्मुखाः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: hitvā atyāyāracitā gṛhāpatyasuhṛt striyaḥ tamo viśantyanicchanto vāsudevaparāṅmukhāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [nominative plural from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ viś class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ viś class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ viś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ viś class 6 verb]

translation: Persons, averse to Lord Vāsudeva, are compelled against their will to abandon their household, children, friends and wealth (property) acquired by them with great efforts and are forced to enter into the dark hell (i.e. ignorance).

devanagari: श्री राजोवाच । कस्मिन् काले स भगवान् किं वर्णः कीदृशो नृभिः । नाम्ना वा केन विधिना पूज्यते तदिहोच्यताम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrī rājovāca kasmin kāle sa bhagavān kiṃ varṇaḥ kīdṛśo nṛbhiḥ nāmnā vā kena vidhinā pūjyate tadihocyatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King Nimi said:
Be pleased to explain to us now, in which ages (yugas) of what complexion and form and by what names is the Lord known and with what rites of worship he is worshipped by men.

devanagari: श्रीकरभाजन उवाच । कृतं त्रेता द्वापरं च कलिरित्येषु केशवः । नानावर्णाभिधाकारो नानैव विधिनेज्यते ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīkarabhājana uvāca kṛtaṃ tretā dvāparaṃ ca kalirityeṣu keśavaḥ nānāvarṇābhidhākāro nānaiva vidhinejyate

translation:
Karabhājana replied:
In the ages Kṛta, Tretā, Dvāpara and Kali, Lord Keśava assumes different forms, complexions and names and he is to be worshipped in different ways.

devanagari: कृते शुक्लश्चतुर्बाहुः जटिलो वल्कलाम्बरः । कृष्णाजिनोपवीताक्षान् बिभ्रद् दण्डकमण्डलू ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛte śuklaścaturbāhuḥ jaṭilo valkalāmbaraḥ kṛṣṇājinopavītākṣān bibhrad daṇḍakamaṇḍalū

translation: In the Kṛta age, the Lord is of fair (white) complexion, has four arms, wears matted locks of hair, is clad in bark-garments and deer-skin; he puts on a sacred thread and carries in his hands a rosary of Rudrākṣa, a staff and a water-jar of gourd.

devanagari: मनुष्यास्तु तदा शान्ता निर्वैराः सुहृदः समाः । यजन्ति तपसा देवं शमेन च दमेन च ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: manuṣyāstu tadā śāntā nirvairāḥ suhṛdaḥ samāḥ yajanti tapasā devaṃ śamena ca damena ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: Men of that Age are quiescent by nature; entertain no feeling of enmity, are friendly towards all and look upon all beings as equals. They worship the Deity with penance, meditation, control of mind and the senses.

devanagari: हंसः सुपर्णो वैकुण्ठो धर्मो योगेश्वरोऽमलः । ईश्वरः पुरुषोऽव्यक्तः परमात्मेति गीयते ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: haṃsaḥ suparṇo vaikuṇṭho dharmo yogeśvaro īśvaraḥ puruṣo
translation: The Lord is addressed with the epithets Haṃsa, Suparṇa, Vaikuṇṭha, Dharma, Lord of Yoga, Amala, Īśvara, Puruṣa Avyakta (the un-manifest) and Paramātman (the Supreme Soul), in that age.

devanagari: त्रेतायां रक्तवर्णोऽसौ चतुर्बाहुस्त्रिमेखलः । हिरण्यकेशः त्रय्यात्मा स्रुक् स्रुवाद्युपलक्षणः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tretāyāṃ raktavarṇo hiraṇyakeśaḥ trayyātmā sruk sruvādyupalakṣaṇaḥ
translation: In the age called Tretā, he the Lord is red in complexion, has four arms and wears a zone of three cords (round his waist indicating initiation), he has golden. locks of hair. He, being the embodiment of sacrifice as described in the three Vedas, carries in his hand sruk, sruvā (ladles and spoons for offering oblations in the fire and other accessories for performing sacrifices.

devanagari: तं तदा मनुजा देवं सर्वदेवमयं हरिम् । यजन्ति विद्यया त्रय्या धर्मिष्ठा ब्रह्मवादिनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tadā manujā devaṃ sarvadevamayaṃ harim yajanti vidyayā trayyā dharmiṣṭhā brahmavādinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: In that age, righteous men who are the expounders of the Vedas, worship Lord Hari, the embodiment of gods, through (rites prescribed in) the three Vedas,

devanagari: विष्णुर्यज्ञः पृश्निगर्भः सर्वदेव उरुक्रमः । वृषाकपिर्जयन्तश्च उरुगाय इतीर्यते ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇuryajñaḥ pṛśnigarbhaḥ sarvadeva urukramaḥ vṛṣākapirjayantaśca urugāya itīryate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yam class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yam class 1 verb]

translation: He is then called Viṣṇu, Yajña, Pṛśnigarbha (the son of Pṛśni, the wife of Prajāpati Sutapas), Sarvadeva, Urukrama, Vṛṣākapi, Jayanta and Urugāya.

devanagari: द्वापरे भगवान् श्यामः पीतवासा निजायुधः । श्रीवत्सादिभिरङ्कैश्च लक्षणैरुपलक्षितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: dvāpare bhagavān śyāmaḥ pītavāsā nijāyudhaḥ śrīvatsādibhiraṅkaiśca lakṣaṇairupalakṣitaḥ
translation: In the Dvāpara age, the Lord is bluish (like the atasī- hemp-flower) in complexion. He wears yellow silken cloth and bears all his original arms (like Sudarśana discus, Kaumodakī mace and others). He is distinguished by his characteristic marks like Śrīvatsa (on his bosom).

devanagari: तं तदा पुरुषं मर्त्या महाराजोपलक्षणम् । यजन्ति वेदतन्त्राभ्यां परं जिज्ञासवो नृप ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tadā puruṣaṃ martyā mahārājopalakṣaṇam yajanti vedatantrābhyāṃ paraṃ jijñāsavo nṛpa

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: In that age, people desirous of knowing the Supreme Brahman worship that Supreme Man characterised by the emblem of a sovereign emperor (white umbrella, chouries [chowries?], etc.) with (the rites prescribed both in) the Vedas and Tantra texts.

devanagari: नमस्ते वासुदेवाय नमः सङ्कर्षणाय च । प्रद्युम्नायानिरुद्धाय तुभ्यं भगवते नमः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: namaste vāsudevāya namaḥ saṅkarṣaṇāya ca pradyumnāyāniruddhāya tubhyaṃ bhagavate namaḥ
translation: Salutations to Vāsudeva, bow to Saṅkarṣaṇa; obeisance to Pradyumna and Aniruddha, to you O glorious Lord (in the four manifestations or Vyūhas).

devanagari: नारायणाय ऋषये पुरुषाय महात्मने । विश्वेश्वराय विश्वाय सर्वभूतात्मने नमः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: nārāyaṇāya ṛṣaye puruṣāya mahātmane viśveśvarāya viśvāya sarvabhūtātmane namaḥ
translation: Hail to the (perfect) sage Nārāyaṇa, the Supreme person, the All-pervading Lord, the Ruler of the Universe, the Indwelling Soul of all created beings.

devanagari: इति द्वापर उर्वीश स्तुवन्ति जगदीश्वरम् । नानातन्त्रविधानेन कलावपि तथा श्रृणु ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti dvāpara urvīśa stuvanti jagadīśvaram nānātantravidhānena kalāvapi tathā śrṛṇu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ stu class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ stu class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ stu class 2 verb]

translation: It is in this manner, O Lord of the earth, that they extol the Ruler of the universe. Now listen to me how people worship him with rites prescribed in various Tantric Texts.

devanagari: कृष्णवर्णं त्विषाकृष्णं साङ्गोपाङ्गास्त्र पार्षदम् । यज्ञैः सङ्कीर्तनप्रायैः यजन्ति हि सुमेधसः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇavarṇaṃ tviṣākṛṣṇaṃ sāṅgopāṅgāstra pārṣadam yajñaiḥ saṅkīrtanaprāyaiḥ yajanti hi sumedhasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ yaj class 1 verb]

translation: It is known to all that highly intelligent and discerning people, through sacrifices mostly consisting of chanting his name and extolling his glory, worship that Lord of dark complexion but brilliant (like Sapphire) in lustre. The Lord who is perfect in every limb, beautified with ornaments (like Kaustubha gem and others), equipped with his weapons and missiles (like the discus Sudarśana) and accompanied with his attendants (like Sunanda and others).

devanagari: ध्येयं सदा परिभवघ्नमभीष्टदोहं । तीर्थास्पदं शिवविरिञ्चिनुतं शरण्यम् । भृत्यार्तिहं प्रणतपाल भवाब्धिपोतं । वन्दे महापुरुष ते चरणारविन्दम...
english_devnagari: dhyeyaṃ sadā paribhavaghnamabhīṣṭadohaṃ tīrthāspadaṃ śivaviriñcinutaṃ śaraṇyam bhṛtyārtihaṃ praṇatapāla bhavābdhipotaṃ vande mahāpuruṣa te caraṇāravindam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ nu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ nu class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ nu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ nu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ nu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ nu class 6 verb]

translation: O Supreme Person! Protector of the votaries (who bow to your feet), I salute your lotus-feet which are even worthy of being meditated upon, which end all the insults and insubordinations (suffered from the members of the family and one’s own sense-organs), bestow all the desired objects, the resort of all sacred places, are praised by god Śiva and Brahmā, an asylum—worthy of taking refuge, annihilate the afflictions of your servants (devotees) and which is a boat for crossing the sea of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: त्यक्त्वा सुदुस्त्यजसुरेप्सित राज्यलक्ष्मीं । धर्मिष्ठ आर्यवचसा यदगात् अरण्यम् । मायामृगं दयितयेप्सितमन्वधावद् । वन्दे महापुरुष ते चरणा...
english_devnagari: tyaktvā sudustyajasurepsita rājyalakṣmīṃ dharmiṣṭha āryavacasā yadagāt araṇyam māyāmṛgaṃ dayitayepsitamanvadhāvad vande mahāpuruṣa te caraṇāravindam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: O the most religious-minded King (Nimi), I bow to the lotus-feet which, at the word of his father (Daśaratha), resigned his prosperous kingdom which was covetable even to gods and hence was very difficult to give up, and departed to the forest, and which gave chase to an illusory deer which was desired by his beloved (Sītā)—To those lotus-feet I bow, O exalted Person.

devanagari: एवं युगानुरूपाभ्यां भगवान् युगवर्तिभिः । मनुजैरिज्यते राजन् श्रेयसा्मीश्वरो हरिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yugānurūpābhyāṃ bhagavān yugavartibhiḥ manujairijyate rājan śreyasā्mīśvaro hariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: In this manner, O King, glorious Lord Hari, who confers all blessings, is worshipped under different names and forms appropriate to the Age (e.g. Kṛta, Tretā) by men belonging to the particular Age.

devanagari: कलिं सभाजयन्त्यार्या गुणज्ञाः सारभागिनः । यत्र सङ्कीर्तनेनैव सर्वस्वार्थोऽभिलभ्यते ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kaliṃ sabhājayantyāryā guṇajñāḥ sārabhāginaḥ yatra saṅkīrtanenaiva sarvasvārtho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [nominative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [instrumental single from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]

translation: Noble discerning persons who know the excellences and essentials, appreciate Kali Age in which all the desired ideals in human life are fully accomplished by merely singing the name and deeds of Hari.

devanagari: न ह्यतः परमो लाभो देहिनां भ्राम्यतामिह । यतो विन्देत परमां शान्तिं नश्यति संसृतिः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: na hyataḥ paramo lābho dehināṃ bhrāmyatāmiha yato vindeta paramāṃ śāntiṃ naśyati saṃsṛtiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: To persons who are wandering in the cycle of Saṃsāra in this world, there is no higher gain than this[3] (glorification and singing the name and deeds of the Lord); for it is by virtue of it, that a person attains sublime serenity and the Saṃsāra in his case comes to an end.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
EK. Poetically explains how four types of muktis (liberation), viz. salokatā, samīpatā, sarūpatā and sāyujyatā are attained during the Kīrtana (glorification of Hari’s names and deeds)—EB 5.425-440.

devanagari: कृतादिषु प्रजा राजन् कलाविच्छन्ति सम्भवम् । कलौ खलु भविष्यन्ति नारायणपरायणाः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtādiṣu prajā rājan kalāvicchanti sambhavam kalau khalu bhaviṣyanti nārāyaṇaparāyaṇāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[ablative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [ablative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Persons belonging to the ages like Kṛta and others (Tretā and Dvāpara) wish to be born in the Kali Age, O King. It is certainly in the Kali age alone that persons exclusively devoted to Lord Nārāyaṇa will be born.

devanagari: क्वचित् क्वचिन्महाराज द्रविडेषु च भूरिशः । ताम्रपर्णी नदी यत्र कृतमाला पयस्विनी ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacit kvacinmahārāja draviḍeṣu ca bhūriśaḥ tāmraparṇī nadī yatra kṛtamālā payasvinī
translation: O great King I such (exclusive devotees of Lord Nārāyaṇa) will be born in some place here and there (in Kali age), but a majority of them will be born in the Dravidian lands wherein flow the rivers, the Tāmraparṇī, the Kṛtamālā, the Payasvinī, the sacred-most Kāverī and the western Mahānadī. People who drink water from these rivers become pure in heart and generally turn into votaries of the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, O ruler of men.

devanagari: कावेरी च महापुण्या प्रतीची च महानदी । ये पिबन्ति जलं तासां मनुजा मनुजेश्वर । प्रायो भक्ता भगवति वासुदेवेऽमलाशयाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kāverī ca mahāpuṇyā pratīcī ca mahānadī ye pibanti jalaṃ tāsāṃ manujā manujeśvara prāyo bhaktā bhagavati vāsudeve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pā class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pā class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ pā class 1 verb]

translation: O great King I such (exclusive devotees of Lord Nārāyaṇa) will be born in some place here and there (in Kali age), but a majority of them will be born in the Dravidian lands wherein flow the rivers, the Tāmraparṇī, the Kṛtamālā, the Payasvinī, the sacred-most Kāverī and the western Mahānadī. People who drink water from these rivers become pure in heart and generally turn into votaries of the glorious Lord Vāsudeva, O ruler of men.

devanagari: देवर्षिभूताप्तनृणां पितॄणां । न किङ्करो नायमृणी च राजन् । सर्वात्मना यः शरणं शरण्यं । गतो मुकुन्दं परिहृत्य कर्तम् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: devarṣibhūtāptanṛṇāṃ pitṝṇāṃ na kiṅkaro nāyamṛṇī ca rājan sarvātmanā yaḥ śaraṇaṃ śaraṇyaṃ gato mukundaṃ parihṛtya kartam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: (With the realisation that Lord Vāsudeva abides everywhere and hence) entertaining no notion of difference (or absolutely giving up all duties prescribed by Śāstraṣ, he, who, with all his heart, seeks resort in Mukunda (Viṣṇu), the shelter of all beings, is under no obligations of gods, sages, creatures, relatives and people, nor is he the servant of any of these. (Hence the performance of the pañca-mahāyajñas which is obligatory for all according to Śāstras, is not applicable to him. He is above the prescriptions and interdictions of the Śāstras).

devanagari: स्वपादमूलं भजतः प्रियस्य । त्यक्तान्यभावस्य हरिः परेशः । विकर्म यच्चोत्पतितं कथञ्चित् । धुनोति सर्वं हृदि सन्निविष्टः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: svapādamūlaṃ bhajataḥ priyasya tyaktānyabhāvasya hariḥ pareśaḥ vikarma yaccotpatitaṃ kathañcit dhunoti sarvaṃ hṛdi sanniviṣṭaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tyaj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: (A devotee stands in no need of expiation for the non-observance of the prescriptions of Dharma). As Lord Hari, the Supreme Ruler, abides in the heart of his devotee who sought shelter at his feet, giving up attachment to his body, etc., (and is not propitiating any other god), the devotee (does not go astray but) if perchance, he commits any sin (due to the force of previous karma), Lord Hari absolves him of all sins.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । धर्मान् भागवतानित्थं श्रुत्वाथ मिथिलेश्वरः । जायन्तेयान् मुनीन् प्रीतः सोपाध्यायो ह्यपूजयत् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvāca dharmān bhāgavatānitthaṃ śrutvātha mithileśvaraḥ jāyanteyān munīn prītaḥ sopādhyāyo hyapūjayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation:
Nārada said:
Thus having listened to the exposition of the course of duties meant for the devotees of the glorious Lord, Nimi, the king of Mithilā, became highly delighted. Accompanied by his family priest, he duly worshipped the nine sages, the sons of Jayantī.

devanagari: ततोऽन्तर्दधिरे सिद्धाः सर्वलोकस्य पश्यतः । राजा धर्मानुपातिष्ठन् अवाप परमां गतिम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: tato rājā dharmānupātiṣṭhan avāpa paramāṃ gatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]

translation: Thereupon, immediately all the Siddhas vanished while all people remained merely looking on. The king also practised the duties (the Bhāgavata way of propitiating the Lord) and attained to the highest state (viz. Mokṣa).

devanagari: त्वमप्येतान् महाभाग धर्मान् भागवतान् श्रुतान् । आस्थितः श्रद्धया युक्तो निःसङ्गो यास्यसे परम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tvamapyetān mahābhāga dharmān bhāgavatān śrutān āsthitaḥ śraddhayā yukto niḥsaṅgo yāsyase param
translation: O highly fortunate Vasudeva! If entertaining no attachment and with devout faith, you follow the Bhāgavata course of conduct, you also will reach the highest goal.

devanagari: युवयोः खलु दम्पत्योः यशसा पूरितं जगत् । पुत्रतामगमद् यद् वां भगवानीश्वरो हरिः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvayoḥ khalu dampatyoḥ yaśasā pūritaṃ jagat putratāmagamad yad vāṃ bhagavānīśvaro hariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pṝ ], [accusative single from √ pṝ ]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The whole world is filled with the fame of you both (Vasudeva and your queen Devakī) as the Supreme Lord Hari is pleased to accept you as his parents (lit. is pleased to be your son).

devanagari: दर्शनालिङ्गनालापैः शयनासनभोजनैः । आत्मा वां पावितः कृष्णे पुत्रस्नेहं प्रकुर्वतोः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: darśanāliṅganālāpaiḥ śayanāsanabhojanaiḥ ātmā vāṃ pāvitaḥ kṛṣṇe putrasnehaṃ prakurvatoḥ
translation: Your soul has been already purified by cherishing parental affection to Kṛṣṇa (as your son) by his sight, embrace, conversation as also by sleeping and sitting with him and by feeding him.

devanagari: वैरेण यं नृपतयः शिशुपालपौण्ड्र । शाल्वादयो गतिविलासविलोकनाद्यैः । ध्यायन्त आकृतधियः शयनासनादौ । तत्साम्यमापुरनुरक्तधियां पुनः किम् ॥ ४८...
english_devnagari: vaireṇa yaṃ nṛpatayaḥ śiśupālapauṇḍra śālvādayo gativilāsavilokanādyaiḥ dhyāyanta ākṛtadhiyaḥ śayanāsanādau tatsāmyamāpuranuraktadhiyāṃ punaḥ kim
translation: If inimical Kings like Śiśupāla, Pauṇḍra, Śālva and others brooded over him with hatred (all the while) while sleeping, sitting, etc. and had their mind transformed, as it was deeply impressed with his gait, graceful activities, glances, etc. and could attain to the semblance of his personality (the Sarūpatā type of liberation), what need be said of those whose mind is deeply attached to him.

devanagari: मापत्यबुद्धिमकृथाः कृष्णे सर्वात्मनीश्वरे । मायामनुष्यभावेन गूढैश्वर्ये परेऽव्यये ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: māpatyabuddhimakṛthāḥ kṛṣṇe sarvātmanīśvare māyāmanuṣyabhāvena gūḍhaiśvarye pare

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle second single], [aorist middle second single]

translation: You should not entertain the notion that Lord Kṛṣṇa is your child; for he is the Supreme Ruler, the soul abiding in all beings, the immutable transcendence who has concealed his Supreme nature by assuming a human form, by the dint of his Māyā potency.

devanagari: भूभारासुरराजन्य हन्तवे गुप्तये सताम् । अवतीर्णस्य निर्वृत्यै यशो लोके वितन्यते ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: bhūbhārāsurarājanya hantave guptaye satām avatīrṇasya nirvṛtyai yaśo loke vitanyate
translation: In order to annihilate the demonic Kṣattriyas who, being veritable Asuras, were a burden to the earth and for the protection of the good and for conferring liberation from Saṃsāra on them, Lord Kṛṣṇa has descended on the earth, and his glory spreads all over the universe.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एतत् श्रृत्वा महाभागो वसुदेवोऽतिविस्मितः । देवकी च महाभागा जहतुः मोहमात्मनः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca etat śrṛtvā mahābhāgo vasudevo devakī ca mahābhāgā jahatuḥ mohamātmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having listened to this, the highly fortunate Vasudeva and the blessed-most Devakī were extremely astonished and the infatuation (of parental affection to Kṛṣṇa) was dissipated (from their mind).

devanagari: इतिहासमिमं पुण्यं धारयेद् यः समाहितः । स विधूयेह शमलं ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: itihāsamimaṃ puṇyaṃ dhārayed yaḥ samāhitaḥ sa vidhūyeha śamalaṃ brahmabhūyāya kalpate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ puṇ class 10 verb]

translation:
Whoever with a concentrated mind understands and retains in memory the sacred account (of the discourses of nine Siddhas) will shake off the illusion (of Saṃsāra) here and become eligible to attain the Brahma-hood.
[back to top]
